notes: Extraction coverage includes Chapter 2, Emergence of the traditional approach; Chapter 3, Early developments in the UK; Chapter 4, Travel forecasting based on discrete choice models, I, including its Notes; and Chapter 5.1–5.8, covering nested logit foundations, the GEV family, model taxonomies, welfare analysis, numerical solution methods, microsimulation, practical disaggregate forecasting applications, stated/revealed preference analysis, further developments in discrete choice analysis including cross-nested logit (CNL), recursive nested extreme value (RNEV), network-GEV, heteroscedastic extreme value (HEV), mixed multinomial logit (MMNL), and hybrid choice models with latent variables, and the chapter conclusion on the evolution and critique of the disaggregate behavioural approach. Chapter 3 Notes and Chapter 4 Notes were explicitly reviewed; no standalone note-only claims were retained where content was supplementary, already represented, uninspected external bibliography, or lacked a faithful controlled predicate.
path: book/Forecasting Urban Travel/Forecasting Urban Travel - Past, Present and Future.md
title: Forecasting Urban Travel: Past, Present and Future
